steps.title: Trace the comparison
steps.content: The poem compares the mind to a flower. A flower flourishes when dew and rain feed it and languishes without them; similarly, the mind flourishes when fed by the "fostering spirit" and languishes without it.

steps.title: Match the shared need
steps.content: A adds a time-based idea that the poem does not present. B treats rain and dew as weather changes rather than nourishment. D says the mind perseveres, but the poem says it languishes without support. The shared point is that both mind and flower need nourishment to thrive. The answer is C.
